dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.4 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

MCP approval prompts now actually surface. When Claude Code calls a gated write tool (cb_slack_post_message, cb_gsheets_update_values, cb_github_create_pr, etc.) the approval banner used to live inside the menu-bar popover only — if the popover was closed (which it always is while you're typing in Claude Code) the prompt silently timed out and the tool came back user_cancelled. The widget now auto-opens the popover and fires a system notification with the tool name + summary every time a new prompt arrives, so the confirm UI is one click away regardless of which app has focus. Approval window widened from 30s to 60s. The notification banner takes a beat to land and you need a moment to read the args before deciding — 30s was cutting it too close, especially when the popover had to open from cold. Backend gate timeout and widget countdown bar are now driven by the same constant so they stay in lock-step. Gate IPC proxy starts at launch. Previously the proxy spawned lazily the first time the popover rendered, so any MCP write call before you'd ever opened the menu would fail with "no emitter configured". It now starts unconditionally from applicationDidFinishLaunching, the same hook the onboarding window uses.
Fixed cb_slack_post_message + every other gated MCP write tool silently timing out when invoked from Claude Code while the popover was closed (issue #11).

Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` scope shipped in 11.3. The cb_gsheets_* tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do. First MCP write call right after launch can still race the gate proxy's IPC handshake by a few hundred ms. If the very first approval prompt never lands, re-run the tool — the second attempt always works because the proxy is warm by then.
